citation.description: Test exists at interfaces/lifecycle/ tier (not unit/graph/) per ticket comment; test_caused_by_link_round_trips calls rebar.link(bug, culprit, 'caused_by') and asserts (culprit, 'caused_by') in _rels(bug, repo).

criteria.criterion: `tests/unit/graph/test_caused_by_relation.py` passes: `rebar link <a> <b> caused_by` exits 0 and the relation appears in the ticket's links.

citation.description: Relation = Literal['blocks', 'depends_on', 'relates_to', 'duplicates', 'supersedes', 'discovered_from', 'caused_by'] — 7 values. Schema at common.schema.json line 17 also has 7-value enum including caused_by.

criteria.criterion: The generated `Relation` literal in `src/rebar/types.py` includes `caused_by` and the schema enum has 7 values.

citation.description: Line 204: `if relation in ('relates_to', 'duplicates', 'supersedes', 'discovered_from', 'caused_by'): return False` — non-blocking/non-cycle. _relations.py _BLOCKING_RELATIONS only has blocks/depends_on. test_caused_by_relation_heldout.py has test_caused_by_is_non_cycle_inducing.

criteria.criterion: `caused_by` is non-blocking and non-cycle-inducing: a unit test asserts a `caused_by` edge does not appear as a blocker in `ready`/graph traversal and a `caused_by` two-node cycle does not raise.

citation.description: link__help.out line 2 shows caused_by in relation list. All overview golden files also show caused_by (confirmed via search_files).

criteria.criterion: The golden CLI-help snapshots are regenerated and the `tests/golden/cli_help` suite passes with `caused_by` listed.

citation.description: test_legacy_relations_still_work parametrized over all 6 legacy relations (blocks, depends_on, relates_to, duplicates, supersedes, discovered_from) asserts they still round-trip correctly after the additive change.

criteria.criterion: A pre-existing LINK event with a legacy relation still reduces without error (back-compat) — unit test asserts unchanged reducer output for a fixture of old links.
